business.title: Jewellery stocks take Rs 60,000 crore hit in 3 days on govt's double-blow. Is the worst behind?
business.summary: Jewellery stocks have plunged up to 20% in three sessions, erasing nearly Rs 60,000 crore in investor wealth after PM Narendra Modi urged households to defer gold purchases and the government hiked import duties. The double blow has rattled sentiment, though analysts see no structural threat to India’s long-term gold demand.

business.title: Cipla Q4 Results: Profit falls 55% YoY to Rs 555 crore; co declares Rs 13/sh dividend
business.summary: Cipla Ltd announced a significant 55% year-on-year drop in its consolidated net profit for the fourth quarter, reaching Rs 555 crore. This contrasts with Rs 1,222 crore reported in the same period last year. The company's Board has recommended a final dividend of Rs 13 per share for the fiscal year ending March 2026.

business.title: Gold import duty hike triggers buying rush and volume concerns, says Senco Gold MD
business.summary: India's gold jewellery market is adjusting to a new reality. An import duty hike is causing consumers to rush purchases. Industry experts predict a drop in sales volumes. Consumers are expected to opt for lighter, lower-value jewellery. This shift could also benefit retailers through higher margins on lower karatage items. Old gold exchanges are likely to increase.

business.title: Iran war to cast a shadow on BRICS foreign ministers meeting in Delhi
business.summary: The main differences have ‌emerged ⁠between Iran and the United Arab Emirates, which are on ⁠opposing sides of the front line in a war launched by the US and Israel on February 28

business.title: The 2013 ghost returns: Will higher gold duties actually restrict imports or just fuel the grey market?
business.summary: India’s shock hike in gold and silver import duty to 15% has revived memories of the 2013 crisis. While the move aims to curb dollar outflows and stabilise the rupee, experts warn it may simply push more demand into the grey market, with limited impact on India’s structural gold appetite.

business.title: Supreme Court stays Madras HC order restraining TVK MLA from floor test
business.summary: The apex court termed the High Court’s reasoning “atrocious” while hearing the petition and also stayed related proceedings

business.title: US to impose visa curbs on 13 people linked to Indian firm over fentanyl
business.summary: This is not the first time that the US government has pulled up KS International Traders. The online pharmacy, along with two Indian nationals, was sanctioned last year ‌for supplying counterfeit fentanyl-laced pills

business.title: Odisha govt asks all departments to be prepared for tackling natural calamities
business.summary: Special Relief Commissioner Rajesh Prabhakar Patil says Odisha remains highly vulnerable to cyclones due to its geographical location along the Bay of Bengal

business.title: Fuel price pass-through needs monitoring as weather risks also posing pressure on inflation: BoB report
business.summary: CPI stays below 4 per cent target in April but food prices and global uncertainties warrant close monitoring by RBI

business.title: HPCL Q4 Results: Net profit jumps 46% YoY to Rs 4,902 crore; Rs 19.25/sh dividend declared
business.summary: Hindustan Petroleum Corporation Limited (HPCL) reported a significant 46% surge in consolidated net profit to Rs 4,902 crore for the fourth quarter of FY26. The oil marketing giant also announced a final dividend of Rs 19.25 per share.  Standalone total income saw a 4.5% year-on-year increase to Rs 1.24 lakh crore during the same period.

business.title: Why gold’s Rs 10,000 spike after import duty increase is a one-off move
business.summary: Gold and silver prices staged a sharp comeback on Wednesday, with MCX futures for both metals surging nearly 6% in a single session after the government raised customs duty on precious metal imports. Gold prices jumped about Rs 9,600, while silver soared nearly Rs 17,000, marking one of the biggest one-day rallies in recent months amid lingering volatility from Middle East tensions and tariff uncertainty.

business.title: Smallcaps rally! IIFL Finance, Hindustan Copper, Meesho, other stocks jump up to 7%
business.summary: Smallcap stocks, including IIFL Finance, Meesho, and GRSE, surged up to 7% on Wednesday, outperforming benchmark indices. This rally occurred despite broader market caution, with the Nifty Smallcap 100 index showing robust gains. Experts remain optimistic about mid and smallcap earnings, viewing recent corrections as opportunities.

business.title: ​US Stock Market: Why investors are betting big on Kopin’s AI future
Bitcoin holds above $80K despite hot US inflation data, signalling strong buyer confidence
business.summary: Bitcoin held above the $80,000 mark despite hotter-than-expected U.S. inflation data, reflecting resilient investor sentiment in the crypto market. While Ethereum and select altcoins traded lower, analysts highlighted improving on-chain activity, institutional inflows and active buyer support, even as macroeconomic uncertainty and interest rate concerns kept broader market sentiment cautious.

business.title: Sensex to hit 89,000? Why Morgan Stanley is betting big on India Inc after 6-quarter slowdown
business.summary: Morgan Stanley has turned more bullish on India, forecasting the BSE Sensex could rise to 89,000 by June 2027, implying around 15% upside from current levels. Strategist Ridham Desai said India is emerging from a six-quarter earnings slowdown into a stronger growth phase after a “mid-cycle” pause.

business.title: Rangasamy takes oath as Puducherry CM for 5th time
business.summary: Lt Governor K Kailashnathan administered the oath of office and secrecy to the CM and his cabinet colleagues.

business.title: Amit Shah cuts convoy size after PM Modi’s fuel conservation appeal
business.summary: The government has also begun integrating electric vehicles into official convoys where feasible.

business.title: Groww shares jump 6% even as Peak XV, two other US investors sell stake worth Rs 5,326 crore. What lies ahead?
business.summary: Billionbrains Garage Ventures shares rebounded on Wednesday, recovering Tuesday’s losses triggered by a Rs 5,326 crore stake sale by Peak XV Partners, Ribbit Capital, and YC Holdings. The investors sold over 29.5 crore shares via bulk deals at an average price of Rs 180.43 apiece, a 1.5% discount to the previous closing price.

business.title: Berger Paints shares soar over 9% after Q4 net profit jumps 27% to Rs 335 crore
business.summary: Berger Paints India saw its shares surge following a robust Q4 FY26 profit jump of over 27%, driven by strong sales volumes and better margins. Despite global uncertainties, the company anticipates continued growth fueled by construction chemicals and new product launches, with price adjustments expected to bolster profitability.

business.title: Japan megabanks to gain access to Anthropic's Mythos in about two weeks, source says
business.summary: Mythos is viewed by ⁠cybersecurity experts as posing significant challenges to the banking industry and its legacy technology systems

business.title: Rising duties may cut India’s gold imports sharply: Mohammed Imran
business.summary: India's bullion market faces a significant reset as the government hikes import duties on gold and jewellery. Analysts predict this will cool demand, reduce imports, and push domestic gold prices higher, potentially impacting jewellery and ETF investments. Meanwhile, silver is poised to benefit from global supply deficits and disruptions.

business.title: Ventive Hospitality Q4 profit climbs 72% to Rs 259 crore, revenue up 21%
business.summary: Ventive Hospitality Ltd announced strong financial results for the fiscal year ending March 31, 2026. Net profit surged 72% in the fourth quarter and full-year profit reached Rs 502 crore. Revenue also saw substantial increases. The company's hotel sector and annuity portfolio performed well. Strategic acquisitions were also completed during the period, positioning Ventive for future growth.

business.title: Market under pressure: IT stocks face long-term headwinds as oil crisis and AI shift weigh on sentiment: Vinit Bolinjkar
business.summary: Vinit Bolinjkar of Ventura Securities advises selling IT stocks. He highlights market risks from the Hormuz Strait crisis, foreign investor selling, as well as the global AI shift. Bolinjkar sees pressure on IT revenues and margins. He prefers Vodafone Idea in telecom. The consumer sector shows surprising strength this earnings season.
